description: This figure presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) line profile analysis results for Fe-6.8Al ferritic low-density steel under three processing conditions: 98% cold-rolled (98%CR, black curves), and isothermally annealed at 750°C for 5 min (red curves) and 60 min (blue curves). Panel (a) displays the normalized intensity of the (110) diffraction peak, showing substantial peak broadening in the 98%CR sample due to lattice micro-strain from severe cold deformation, with progressive peak sharpening upon annealing indicating recrystallization; the inset shows complete XRD profiles confirming the single-phase ferrite (BCC) structure with indexed reflections including (110), (200), (211), (220), and (310). Panels (b), (c), and (d) present the variance method analysis, plotting the second-order moment (M₂, solid lines) and normalized fourth-order moment (M₄/q², dashed lines) as functions of the scattering vector q for the 98%CR, 750°C/5min, and 750°C/60min conditions, respectively, used to separate size and strain contributions. Panel (e) shows the calculated dislocation density evolution, revealing a dramatic three-order-of-magnitude decrease from approximately 2×10¹⁵ m⁻² in the cold-rolled state to ~10¹² m⁻² after 60 min annealing, confirming that recrystallization drastically reduces dislocation density and lattice micro-strain (from 1.46×10⁻³ to 1.46×10⁻⁴).
